Question: Solve the system of equations in three variables. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + y -2 z = -1 \\ 3 x -3 y - z = 5 \\ x -2 y + 3 z = 6\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system of three equations in three variables. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\text{} x + y + z = 2 \\ \text{} y -3 z = 1 \\ 2 x + y + 5 z = 0\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the following system. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\text{} x + y + z = 7 \\ 3 x - 2 y - z = 4 \\ x + 6 y + 5 z = 24\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: Infinite number of solutions of the form $$( x , 4 x -11 , -5 x + 18 ) .$$
